title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
summary: Gabe De Mesa from OpenGov presents lessons from building and scaling OG Assist, an AI agent product serving thousands of state and local governments. The talk covers the core agent loop harness, feedback and evals, long context handling, monitoring, tool building, and production deployment best practices using Effect-TS, Claude, and Cursor.
writeup: Gabe De Mesa of OpenGov details how the company built and scaled OG Assist, an AI agent product serving thousands of state and local governments. The talk walks through production architecture: the core agent loop harness, feedback and evals, long context handling, monitoring and observability, tool building, and deployment practices, implemented with Effect-TS, Claude, and Cursor. It also touches the A2A protocol and test-time compute. The focus is on operating agents at scale in a real production setting rather than prototypes.
